item.title: Vimeo Parent Bending Spoons Files for US IPO Showing Sales Jump
item.description: Bending Spoons, which owns digital applications like Vimeo, Evernote and AOL, filed for an initial public offering, joining a string of European tech companies seeking US listings.

item.title: The Global Economic Toll of Oil Prices at $120 for an Entire Year
item.description: The impact is particularly pronounced for emerging economies that rely on the Middle East for oil, petroleum, gas and fertilizer.

item.title: Apple Investors Look for AI Overhaul to Power Next Leg of Gains
item.description: Apple Inc. investors have spent nearly two years clamoring for the iPhone maker to make a big splash with artificial intelligence. Their wait may finally be coming to an end next week at the company’s annual Worldwide Developers Conference.

item.title: Apple’s Revamped AI Is a Long-Term Play in a Down PC, Mobile Phone Market
item.description: A revived Siri unlikely to spur iPhone, Mac sales in the near future

item.title: Trump’s Tariff Wall Takes a Curious Woke Turn
item.description: A president known for calling climate change a hoax is threatening Brazil with tariffs over deforestation

item.title: Uber Opens London Waitlist for Wayve Robotaxis Ahead of Launch
item.description: Uber Technologies Inc. has set up a waitlist for customers interested in riding in a robotaxi in London, ahead of a planned commercial debut in the UK this year.

item.title: Clean-Tech Venture Investors Face Concentration Risks in Funds
item.description: Investors in early-stage clean technologies in the US and Europe may be less diversified than they realize.

item.title: Bulls Make Their Case as Stock Risks Pile Up
item.description: The sudden pullback is focusing attention on rising oil prices, interest rates and AI momentum.

item.title: US Tech Workers Are Paying the Price of the AI Boom
item.description: The information sector is dominant in profit expansion — and one of the worst places to find work.

item.title: Private Credit Performance Defies Media Gloom, Arcmont CEO Says
item.description: Institutional investors are showing faith in private credit even as retail money gets spooked, since default rates in portfolios remain low, according to Anthony Fobel, chief executive officer of Arcmont Asset Management.

item.title: France Faces New Era of Slumping Births and Demographic Decline
item.description: France’s population is on course for rapid decline and aging that will further weigh on an economy already struggling with slowing growth and a rising debt mountain.

item.title: Nvidia, Hyundai Deepen Joint Push Into AI-Powered Robotics
item.description: Nvidia Corp. and Hyundai Motor Group agreed to deepen their alliance to turn so-called physical AI and robotics into real industrial products.

item.title: Indonesia Weighs Export Rule Exemptions for Commodity Traders
item.description: Indonesia is discussing carve-outs from sweeping new export controls that could allow commodity traders to sidestep all or part of the rules in exchange for investments in the country and joint ventures with a newly established state body.

item.title: Korean Leveraged ETF Misfires, Jumps 50% Even as SK Hynix Slumps
item.description: A leveraged exchange-traded fund tied to SK Hynix Inc. surged 50% on Monday despite a drop of nearly 8% in the underlying stock, a dislocation the fund house later attributed to a lack of liquidity in its market-making system.

item.title: Universal Music Sounds Out Investors for €1 Billion Bond Sale
item.description: Universal Music Group NV is speaking to investors ahead of a possible €1 billion ($1.15 billion) two-part bond sale on Monday, after rejecting hedge fund billionaire Bill Ackman’s takeover offer.

item.title: Blackstone Looks to Sell $2 Billion of Fund Stakes, FT Says
item.description: Blackstone Inc. is looking to sell more than $2 billion of its stakes in private investment funds by bundling the exposure into bonds for investors, the Financial Times reported.

item.title: Wall Street Strategists See Rally Intact After Healthy Tech Drop
item.description: Strategists at some of Wall Street’s biggest banks are looking past Friday’s tech-led selloff, saying earnings growth should support stocks through the end of the year.

item.title: UAE Sells Bumper Gulf Oil Volumes for Loading Over Coming Months
item.description: Abu Dhabi National Oil Co. is selling a bumper volume of crude originating from inside the Persian Gulf to traders and refiners in Asia, in the first tenders of their kind for such grades since the Iran war began.

item.title: Why Trump’s War Hasn’t Broken Iran’s Economy
item.description: Iran’s economy was already under strain before the US and Israel launched their war on the Islamic Republic. It was grappling with negative growth, soaring inflation and a currency crisis. Those problems have been compounded by strikes on the country’s civilian and industrial infrastructure and the US naval blockade of Iranian ports.

item.title: Tencent Aims to Raise $3 Billion in Dollar, Yuan Bond Offering
item.description: Tencent Holdings Ltd. is preparing to raise around $3 billion in a dual-currency bond offering that could be priced as early as Tuesday, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Indonesia Has No Cabinet Reshuffle Plan for Now, Official Says
item.description: Indonesia has no plan to revamp its cabinet as of now, Minister of State Secretary Prasetyo Hadi said on Monday.

item.title: Singapore’s Wong Warns of Growth, Inflation Risks in Second Half
item.description: Singapore Prime Minister Lawrence Wong warned that the economy has yet to feel the full impact of the Middle East conflict, and uncertainty remains over the growth outlook despite a strong performance in the first quarter.

item.title: Qatar Quietly Sends LNG Tanker Through Hormuz Despite Tensions
item.description: Another liquefied natural gas shipment from Qatar transited the Strait of Hormuz over the weekend, according to ship data, even as tension in the region threatens a broader peace deal between the US and Iran.

item.title: Chile’s Tren de Aragua Probe Revives Bank Secrecy Debate
item.description: The June arrest of a bank employee accused of ties to Venezuela’s Tren de Aragua criminal organization has reignited debate over Chile’s financial secrecy rules, which are among the strictest in the world.

item.title: Cosco to Use New Spanish Port Terminal to Import Cars From China
item.description: Cosco Shipping Holdings Co. will use a terminal it acquired in eastern Spain mainly for car imports, underlying the country’s growing importance for Chinese automobile manufacturers.

item.title: Schroders Shuns Treasuries and Bunds in Favor of Italian Debt
item.description: Asset manager Schroders is buying Italian government bonds, arguing the country has already weathered the budget and political turmoil now rattling investors in other European nations and the US.

item.title: Record Fine Hits Romanian Bank Stocks After Interbank Rate Probe
item.description: Romania’s largest bank shares tumbled after the nation’s competition watchdog imposed a record fine over alleged collusion in setting money-market rates.

item.title: Private Markets Elite Gather in Berlin Amid Not-So-Super Returns
item.description: Private market dealmakers attending SuperReturn in Berlin this week are likely to be handed a short but sharp to-do list by their investors: give us our money back.

item.title: RBI Seen Joining Asia’s Rate-Hike Push as Inflation Risks Rise
item.description: The Reserve Bank of India will likely tighten monetary policy in coming months, bringing it in line with regional peers that have turned more hawkish in response to inflationary pressures stemming from the Middle East conflict, economists said.

item.title: India Bond Inflows Hit One-Year High After Rupee Support Steps
item.description: India’s index-eligible bonds recorded their largest inflow in almost a year after authorities announced measures aimed at boosting foreign purchases of the nation’s debt and supporting the rupee.
